items.title: Flex alignment and order: reject the invalid model
items.prompt: Which statement correctly resolves this CSS: Responsive and Accessible Styling misconception? flex:1 guarantees equal final widths regardless of content and minimum size.
hints: Compare both options with platform semantics and observable user or browser evidence.

feedback.correct: Correct. Account for basis and intrinsic minimums; flex growth alone does not guarantee equal final sizes.
feedback.incorrect: That preserves the misconception. Account for basis and intrinsic minimums; flex growth alone does not guarantee equal final sizes.

criteria.description: Selects the source-linked CSS: Responsive and Accessible Styling correction rather than the documented misconception.

options.text: Account for basis and intrinsic minimums; flex growth alone does not guarantee equal final sizes.

answer.explanation: Account for basis and intrinsic minimums; flex growth alone does not guarantee equal final sizes.

items.title: Flex alignment and order: verified implementation facet
items.prompt: Align items and groups while avoiding visual order that contradicts DOM and keyboard order. Build a bounded artifact demonstrating alignment without contradicting source order; the locked browser verifier checks observable structure, behavior, or computed presentation.

examEligibility.rationale: AI-assisted authoring draft. Runtime evidence does not replace independent human standards, pedagogy, accessibility, answer-oracle, and formal-exam review.

hints: Implement the smallest artifact that satisfies the declared observable contract, then check the visible case before considering the hidden boundary.

feedback.correct: The locked authoring verifier observed the declared bounded facet in both visible and hidden cases.
feedback.incorrect: At least one deterministic authoring check failed; inspect the exact artifact, interaction state, viewport, and observable result without assuming hidden-case details.

criteria.description: Passes the visible and hidden deterministic checks for the declared implementation facet in its pinned authoring environment.

privateAuthorNotes: This code item and its oracle are AI-assisted draft material with reviewer=null and examEligibility=false.
Browser-verifier items are authoring evidence only and must never be routed to the official untrusted-code runner or formal exam pipeline.
items.starterCode: /* TODO: implement the declared bounded CSS facet. */

answer.referenceSolution: .panel { display: flex; flex-flow: row wrap; gap: 1rem; align-items: center; justify-content: space-between; } .copy { flex: 1 1 20rem; } .action { order: 0; }
answer.explanation: The reference is a bounded css artifact for alignment without contradicting source order. Passing proves only the declared observable facet, not holistic mastery or human accessibility approval.
